chunk_summary: Diarrhea management in pediatric nutrition integrates ORS and zinc supplementation to prevent malnutrition, emphasizing continued feeding, breastfeeding, and operational integration for better outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of ORS and zinc in managing pediatric diarrhea and preventing malnutrition.
Learn WHO-recommended zinc dosages for children under and over 6 months.
Recognize the importance of continued feeding and breastfeeding during diarrhea episodes.
Identify operational strategies to integrate diarrhea management with nutrition care.
key_facts: WHO recommends zinc supplementation for 10–14 days: 10 mg/day for infants under 6 months and 20 mg/day for children 6 months and above.
Zinc supplementation should continue even after stools normalize due to its extended protective effects.
ORS–zinc corners and community counseling structures are institutionalized in national diarrhea control programs.
Severe acute malnutrition (SAM) requires careful rehydration with supervised ORS use and monitoring for overhydration.
Persistent diarrhea in stunted or wasted children should trigger reassessment for infection and feeding tolerance.
reference_quote: WHO recommends zinc for 10–14 days: 10 mg/day for infants under 6 months and 20 mg/day for children 6 months and above. (Source: https://www.who.int/tools/elena/interventions/zinc-diarrhoea)
